summary: Round 2 re-validation: PASS, zero findings. Both round-1 minor partials are now resolved. (1) rate=0 busy-loop: Decision 2 now explicitly reconciles the user-spec edge case (lines 145-146) — unlimited is a deliberate max-rate send loop doing real work each iteration (a PREPARE per iteration), pegging ~1 core, explicitly distinguished from the pathological empty busy-spin the user-spec warned against; the resolution now lives in a Decision, not only the Risks table. (2) Large plan-size caveat: Task 3 (README) now carries the instruction to document that a very large plan-size makes each PREPARE heavy/slow (user-spec edge case lines 135-136). All 14 user-spec acceptance criteria map fully to tech-spec coverage. New security/arch-review items reviewed for scope creep and confirmed as justified engineering safeguards, NOT new functionality: Decision 8 (application_name=noisia on the dedicated connection) restores parity with the pool path (db.NewPostgresDB already sets it) for incident attribution + demo teaching value, tracing to the convention-following constraint; Decision 9 (no Conninfo in any log/wrapped error, CWE-532 hardening) directly implements user-spec AC 'Conninfo nowhere logged' (line 172) and the secret-leak risk; separate report-ticker goroutine (Architecture step 4 / Decision 6) implements the user-spec UX requirement that the panel stays informative when the DB is down (lines 112-113) and resolves pgx Conn single-use concurrency safety — implementation strategy for an existing requirement; first-Exec guard (Decision 5, counter>0) is an underengineering safeguard separating a builder bug from a real OOM climax, supporting the user-spec setup-failure-vs-mid-run-climax distinction (lines 130-131, AC line 167). Reverse traceability: every tech-spec element traces to a requirement or is acceptable infra (tests, README, CLI wiring, error handling, attribution, log sanitization) — no scope creep. Right-sizing: NOT overengineered — Decisions 2,3,5,6 each reuse a single loop/code path and reject the more complex alternatives (no pgx error typing, no external monitoring polling, no separate unlimited branch, no procfs/cgroup auto plan-size); the separate ticker goroutine is the minimum needed to keep the panel firing under memory pressure on a non-concurrency-safe Conn, not a speculative layer. NOT underengineered — connect failure, mid-run PREPARE error, first-Exec builder-bug guard, connection-loss climax, ctx cancel, show-memory read failure, PG<14 degradation, and conninfo leakage are all handled. Solution and Architecture have real technical substance (concrete wide-literal PREPARE builder, rate.Inf single-path mechanism, single-connection rationale, atomic-published memory value). Inline Implementation Tasks (4) are brief scope descriptions with files/verify and no pseudocode; Decisions are self-contained in the Decisions section (no decision-level content stranded only in task descriptions). No critical, major, or minor findings -> pass.
